response:
Certainly! Below are the unit tests for the `is_valid_email` function using `pytest`. These tests cover the happy path, edge cases, and error cases.

```python
import pytest
from your_module import is_valid_email  # Replace 'your_module' with the actual module name where the function is defined

def test_valid_email():
    # Happy path: Valid emails
    assert is_valid_email("test@example.com") == True
    assert is_valid_email("user.name+tag+sorting@example.com") == True
    assert is_valid_email("user.name@subdomain.example.com") == True
    assert is_valid_email("user.name@domain.com") == True
    assert is_valid_email("user.name@domain.co.uk") == True

def test_invalid_email():
    # Edge cases and error cases: Invalid emails
    assert is_valid_email("") == False  # Empty string
    assert is_valid_email("user@") == False  # Missing domain
    assert is_valid_email("@example.com") == False  # Missing local part
    assert is_valid_email("user@.com") == False  # Missing domain name
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain") == False  # Missing top-level domain
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain..com") == False  # Double dot in domain
    assert is_valid_email("user@-domain.com") == False  # Domain starting with a hyphen
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain-.com") == False  # Domain ending with a hyphen
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.c") == False  # Single character TLD
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.123") == False  # TLD with numbers
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain..com") == False  # Double dot in domain
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com.") == False  # Trailing dot in domain
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com..") == False  # Double trailing dot
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com/a") == False  # Extra characters after TLD
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com a") == False  # Extra characters after TLD
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com a") == False  # Extra characters after TLD
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com a") == False  # Extra characters after TLD
